methods.description: This method describes the procedure for conducting an experimental run to monitor signal changes in response to different conditions, including baseline signal establishment, cell loading, and the addition of reagents.

steps.description: The experimental run was started by pumping fresh ASW over an acid-washed 25-mm 0.45-µm cellulose acetate filter for ~4 min to get a steady baseline signal (region 1 in Figure S2), R ASW.
steps.searchable_summary: Pump fresh ASW over filter to establish baseline signal

steps.description: The pump was then briefly stopped (< 3 s) while the tubing was switched to the culture.
steps.searchable_summary: Switch tubing to culture after stopping pump briefly

steps.description: The cells were loaded onto the filter through the pump, which took less than 2 min (region 2 in Figure S2).
steps.searchable_summary: Load cells onto filter through pump

steps.description: The pump was again briefly stopped (< 3 s) while the tubing was switched back to the ASW.
steps.searchable_summary: Switch tubing back to ASW after stopping pump briefly

steps.description: The signal was then monitored for ~10 min (region 3 in Figure S2), with R cell obtained as the average of the 500 counts taken at the end of this time period.
steps.searchable_summary: Monitor signal and calculate R cell as average of 500 counts

steps.description: A O2 spike was then added to the ASW from a freshly prepared working stock solution, as described for the calibration procedure above, and the signal was monitored for ~5 min (region 4 in Figure S2).
steps.searchable_summary: Add O2 spike to ASW and monitor signal

steps.description: Finally, superoxide dismutase (SOD) was added to the ASW at a final concentration of ~0.24 U mL-1 (~1.6 nM) (region 5 in Figure S2). R SOD was obtained as the average of 100 counts taken as soon as the signal stabilized after the SOD addition.
steps.searchable_summary: Add SOD to ASW and calculate R SOD as average of 100 counts
